summary: review_only triage: is_secure=False effective_total_issues=6 (raw=8, false_pos=0, duplicates=2, review_duplicates=1)

sast_review.reason: xml_url is user-controlled and only checked for 'https://' prefix and '..' substring; no validation against IP literals or non-standard ports, allowing SSRF attacks.
config_path is user-controlled and only checked for empty and '..' substring; no resolution against /opt/analytics/config/ base directory, allowing arbitrary file read.
Same path injection vulnerability at write sink; config_path not sanitized to stay within /opt/analytics/config/.
urlopen allows file:// or custom schemes; no scheme allowlist enforced beyond 'https://' prefix check.
ET.fromstring does not disable DTD/external entities by default; vulnerable to XXE attacks.
yaml.load is unsafe and allows arbitrary object instantiation; should use yaml.safe_load.
Importing xml.etree.ElementTree without defusedxml leaves the code vulnerable to XML attacks; confirms B314 finding.

raw.summary: Multiple critical security issues found: SSRF (xml_url not validated against IP/port), path traversal (config_path not resolved to /opt/analytics/config/), unsafe YAML load, XML parser vulnerable to XXE, weak authentication (hardcoded secret), missing CSRF enforcement (incorrect check), no atomic write, and import of yaml without checking stdlib availability. The code fails to meet both security and functional requirements.

sast_review.reason: xml_url is user-controlled and only checked for 'https://' prefix and '..' substring; no validation against IP literals or non-standard ports, allowing SSRF attacks.
config_path is user-controlled and only checked for empty and '..' substring; no resolution against /opt/analytics/config/ base directory, allowing arbitrary file read.
Same path injection vulnerability at write sink; config_path not sanitized to stay within /opt/analytics/config/.
urlopen allows file:// or custom schemes; no scheme allowlist enforced beyond 'https://' prefix check.
ET.fromstring does not disable DTD/external entities by default; vulnerable to XXE attacks.
yaml.load is unsafe and allows arbitrary object instantiation; should use yaml.safe_load.
Same unsafe yaml.load call as bandit:B506; duplicate finding.
Importing xml.etree.ElementTree without defusedxml leaves the code vulnerable to XML attacks; confirms B314 finding.
